response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished generalist software engineer, but the profile does not match the core requirements of this role. The only substantive C++ exposure is roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a university thesis project — far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience (only MySQL/general SQL), and no fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain background. RIDE Capital is the closest financial touchpoint, but that work was Node/TypeScript/GraphQL, not C++ in an investment-banking context.
strengths: Broad, deep software engineering experience across web, backend, and embedded systems
Strong testing/craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development)
Some fintech exposure at RIDE Capital and low-latency, high-throughput systems work
concerns: Minimal C++ experience (~6 months) versus the required 5–10 years on Windows
No SQL Server/Oracle and no fixed income or capital-markets domain knowledge
Recent stack is TypeScript/Node/DLang, not aligned with the role's technical core
